sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (event_time DateTime64(9), id UInt32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(event_time) AS rounded_time, (toStartOfMicrosecond(event_time) % 2) AS mod_result FROM test_dates ORDER BY mod_result;

WITH (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE id % 2 = 0) AS even_count SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(event_time) AS rounded_time, EVEN(count()) AS average_even_count FROM test_dates GROUP BY rounded_time HAVING average_even_count > 1;
CREATE TABLE test_numbers (num Float64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY num;
SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(toDateTime64(CAST(num AS String), 9)) AS microsecond_start, (toStartOfMicrosecond(toDateTime64(CAST(num AS String), 9)) % 3) AS num_mod FROM test_numbers;

WITH (SELECT MIN(num) FROM test_numbers) AS min_num SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(DATE_ADD('2023-10-06 12:05:00', INTERVAL min_num SECOND)) AS calculated_time, (calculated_time % 5) AS mod_value FROM (SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(DATE_ADD('2023-10-06 12:05:00', INTERVAL num SECOND)) AS calculated_time FROM test_numbers);
CREATE TABLE test_complex (create_time DateTime64(3), num_id UInt32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY num_id;
SELECT toStartOfMicrosecond(create_time) AS rounded_time, (toStartOfMicrosecond(create_time) % 10) AS mod_result FROM test_complex WHERE mod_result IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (toStartOfMicrosecond(event_time) % (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates)) AS mod_count FROM test_dates WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL ORDER BY mod_count;
